title: Merging Election Data
text: Many sources electoral data focused, expected, elections. , many lack population data can necessary place racial, turnout, population patterns context. One best sources electoral data Voting Election Science Team spatial analysis. Using one county Virginia senate election 2018, example walks easy way add population data matching based geographies. First, load VA county subset included package. noting countiy included, can use create_block_table collect key population boxes needed redistricting analysis. avoid needing API access, can use saved data. polygons simplified using rmapshaper. typical approach match geography blocks precincts. case, geographies blocks expected match well one precinct match block. necessarily case states data sources, blindly matching can dangerous. , use default matching, st_centerish, centroid shape within shape point surface otherwise. three options used: - centroid: center mass shape - point: point surface shape - area: intersection largest area (useful expect boundaries don’t line well) Using centroid matching: can aggregate precinct level block2prec. alternative way , especially edges counties follow natural geographies like rivers, use block2prec_by_county, performs two steps, one county time. returns tables joined together. Since one county example, unnecessary. case, makes difference, va18sub shapes line well block lines. either case, data arranged order second argument (/precinct), can column bind full dataset analysis purposes.
code: data(va18sub) unique(va18sub$COUNTYFP) ## [1] "087" block <- create_block_table(state = 'VA', county = '087', year = 2010) data("va_blocks") block <- va_blocks matches <- geo_match(from = block, to = va18sub, method = 'centroid') prec <- block2prec(block_table = block, matches = matches) prec_by_c <- block2prec_by_county(block_table = block, precinct = va18sub, precinct_county_fips = 'COUNTYFP') fulldata <- bind_cols(va18sub, prec)

Now, always hopeful bit lines generally close , final quality estimate fairly good. Next, need vector matches precinct data (va18sub) blocks. similarly, vector matches voting district data (vtd) blocks. Suppose want estimate votes Tim Kaine, weighting VAP. can reverse get vtd level: Generally, want multiple variables, recommend using geo_match() identify matches , can apply estimate_down() estimate_up() steps variable. However, cases need transfer one variable concerned simplicity time, following perform steps: estimate va18sub blocks blocks vtd , two methods give answers, first approach allow apply logic many variables.
code: data(va18sub) block <- create_block_table(state = 'VA', county = '087') vtd <- tt_voting_districts(state = 'VA', county = '087', year = 2010) data("va_vtd") vtd <- va_vtd vtd |> ggplot() +   geom_sf() +    geom_sf(data = va18sub, color = 'red', fill = 'NA' )+   theme_void() matches_p <- geo_match(from = block, to = va18sub, method = 'centroid') matches_v <- geo_match(from = block, to = vtd, method = 'centroid') disagg_kaine <- estimate_down(wts = block$vap, value = va18sub$G18USSDKAI, group = matches_p) vtd_kaine <- estimate_up(value = disagg_kaine, group = matches_v) disagg_kaine_geo <- geo_estimate_down(from = va18sub, to = block, wts = block$vap,                                        value = va18sub$G18USSDKAI, method = 'centroid')  vtd_kaine_geo <- geo_estimate_up(from = block, to = vtd, value = disagg_kaine_geo, method =                                     'centroid') all(disagg_kaine == disagg_kaine_geo) & all(vtd_kaine == vtd_kaine_geo) ## [1] TRUE

Columns include group Values inputted group argument. group specified, values 1. group_number Integer encoding group. component number contiguous set units within group. units within group contiguous, values 1. two sets, discontiguous , larger one numbered 1 smaller one numbered 2.

title: Check Contiguity by Group — check_contiguity
text: Given zero-indexed adjacency list array group identifiers, returns tibble identifies connected components. three columns group inputted group, group_number uniquely identifies group positive integer, component identifies connected component number corresponding entry adjacency group. everything connected within group, element component 1. Otherwise, largest component given value 1, next largest 2, . nothing provided group, default vector ones, checking adjacency graph connected. cct() shorthand creating table component values. everything connected within group, returns value 1. general, returns frequency table components. ccm() shorthand getting maximum component value. returns maximum number  components group broken . returns 1 group connected. #'

title: Match Features Across Geographic Layers — geo_match
text: sf object match , usually smaller geography. sf object match , usually larger geography. method Matching method. One "center", "centroid", "point", "circle", "area". Optional character scalar restricting matching within shared groups. Use single value use column name, named scalar c(county_from = "county_to") column names differ. tiebreaker Logical. TRUE, ambiguous unmatched features assigned nearest feature . FALSE, unmatched rows receive -1 rows multiple matches receive -2. epsg numeric EPSG code planarize . Default 3857.

title: Match Features Across Geographic Layers — geo_match
text: integer vector length nrow(). Values row indices , -1 / -2 tiebreaker = FALSE. Empty geometries returned NA.

title: Match Features Across Geographic Layers — geo_match
text: Methods follows: "centroid": match using geometric centroid row . "center": use centroid lies inside polygon otherwise fall back point--surface. "point": use point surface row . "circle": use centroid maximum inscribed circle. "area": match row largest area overlap. output reference every row , attribute "matching_max" attached records nrow(). Functions block2prec() use attribute preserve empty target groups.
